coreJobStatement: Raise, house, feed, and condition horses in managed facilities so that animals remain healthy, tractable, and available for their designated use — breeding, sport, recreation, or instruction
hierarchy: End-Beneficiary Process VN (L7 through L4) — equine husbandry facility operations in DACH region
marketSize: ~EUR 3–8M hay rack sub-segment DACH [EST]; full L6c feeding integrator TAM ~EUR 50–100M DACH

l6Systems.name: Facility Infrastructure & Site Preparation
l6Systems.jobFamily: Establish and maintain the physical environment required to house and operate a horse facility
l6Systems.scope: Stable buildings, riding arenas, hard standing, drainage, ventilation — permanent infrastructure

l6Systems.name: Animal Intake & Identity Management
l6Systems.jobFamily: Register, assess, and onboard incoming animals; establish individual health and nutrition baseline
l6Systems.scope: EU equine passports (Reg. 504/2008), RFID microchip, veterinary intake assessment, digital management software

l6Systems.name: Daily Feeding & Nutrition Management
l6Systems.jobFamily: Deliver correct type, quantity, and timing of feed to each animal while minimizing waste and contamination
l6Systems.scope: Outdoor hay rack stations, grain dispensers, automatic waterers, feed storage — universal across all output types

l6Systems.name: Housing & Paddock Space Management
l6Systems.jobFamily: Configure, operate, and reconfigure stable boxes, paddocks, and outdoor exercise areas for daily equine welfare and workflow
l6Systems.scope: Mobile panel systems, stable stalls, bedding management — universal across all output types

l6Systems.name: Health & Veterinary Care
l6Systems.jobFamily: Maintain animal health through preventive protocols, farriery, and veterinary treatment
l6Systems.scope: Farriery equipment, veterinary stocks, health monitoring — all output types

l6Systems.name: Manure & Waste Management
l6Systems.jobFamily: Remove, store, and dispose of dung and soiled bedding daily to meet Tierschutz and biosecurity standards
l6Systems.scope: Manure collection, storage bunker, spreader/disposal — all output types

l6Systems.name: Training & Conditioning
l6Systems.jobFamily: Develop and maintain fitness, responsiveness, and gait quality in horses for their designated purpose
l6Systems.scope: Lunging, mechanical walkers, rider training aids — Reitstall, Reitschule, Turnierstall only

l6Systems.name: Breeding & Reproduction
l6Systems.jobFamily: Manage estrus cycles, breeding events, gestation, and foaling

l6Systems.name: Records, Compliance & Tierschutz
l6Systems.jobFamily: Maintain EU equine passport records, Tierschutzgesetz feeding logs, facility compliance documentation
l6Systems.scope: Mandatory across all core steps — EU Reg. 504/2008, German Tierschutzgesetz, DIN 18910 stable requirements

l6Systems.name: Facility Maintenance
l6Systems.jobFamily: Structural repairs, equipment servicing, surface maintenance — required for all L6 core steps to run safely
l6Systems.scope: Annual inspection schedule, reactive maintenance, planned replacement of wearing equipment

vnUnits.name: Stable Building / Barn Structure
vnUnits.functionalJob: Permanent weatherproof enclosure for horse stalls. Alternatives: prefab steel hall (~45%), timber barn (~35%), masonry construction (~20%).
vnUnits.description: Permanent capital investment. Long lifecycle (20–50 years). Procured once during facility development.

vnUnits.name: Riding Arena Surface & Enclosure
vnUnits.functionalJob: Exercise surface and perimeter for daily training. Sand/fibre (~55%), natural turf (~30%), synthetic Equisand (~15%).
vnUnits.description: Major capital item. Arena surface requires periodic top-up and maintenance.

vnUnits.name: Equine Identification System
vnUnits.functionalJob: EU passport + RFID microchip mandatory per EU Reg. 504/2008. Digital management software (StableMate, eHorse) for records management.
vnUnits.description: 100% penetration — EU mandatory. Software adoption growing. Near-zero competitive differentiation.

vnUnits.name: Multi-Horse Outdoor Hay Rack / Station — BÖHMER HEURAUFE PRIMARY POSITION
vnUnits.functionalJob: Roofed, galvanized, multi-access outdoor hay feeding station; tractor-movable. Alternatives: roofed hay rack (~20% BÖHMER PRIMARY), round bale ring feeder (~35%), wall-mounted corner feeder (~25%), hay net station (~15%), RFID dispenser (~5%).
vnUnits.description: Böhmer Heuraufe PRIMARY anchor position. Key differentiator: only roofed, multi-access, tractor-movable category. Universal across all 5 output types. BÖHMER PRIMARY

vnUnits.name: Grain & Concentrate Dispensing
vnUnits.functionalJob: Individual or shared feed trough/bucket for concentrate delivery. Manual trough/bucket (~55%), RFID automatic station (~30%), semi-auto dispenser (~15%).
vnUnits.description: High-frequency daily operation. RFID dispensing growing due to individual feeding precision. Not in Böhmer scope.

vnUnits.name: Automatic Waterers
vnUnits.functionalJob: Continuous frost-protected water access. Individual heated ball-valve drinker (~50%), shared heated trough (~35%), frost-free tank (~15%).
vnUnits.description: Critical welfare equipment. Individual drinkers growing due to water consumption monitoring capability.

vnUnits.name: Feed Storage Infrastructure
vnUnits.functionalJob: Hay barn or covered storage enclosure for bulk hay and concentrate. Hay barn (~50%), grain silo/bins (~35%), mobile container (~15%).
vnUnits.description: Böhmer adjacency candidate (L5): hay barn steel frame sub-assembly within welded structural steel competence. MEDIUM adaptation effort.

vnUnits.name: Mobile Modular Panel System — BÖHMER PADDOCKBOX PRIMARY POSITION
vnUnits.functionalJob: Galvanized steel tube-frame panels on heavy-duty castors; repositionable without disassembly; wall-hinge variant available. Alternatives: mobile panel system (~15% BÖHMER PRIMARY), permanent post-and-rail (~45%), electric tape fencing (~25%), V-mesh wire (~10%), aluminium portable panels (~5%).
vnUnits.description: Böhmer Paddockbox PRIMARY anchor position. Key differentiator: only castor-mounted, fold-flat, wall-attachable category. Universal across all 5 output types. BÖHMER PRIMARY

vnUnits.name: Stable Stall Equipment (interior box stalls)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Fixed or modular interior horse stalls. Traditional timber box (~50%), prefab steel modular stall (~35%), open-front/group housing (~15%).
vnUnits.description: HIGH-VALUE ADJACENCY: prefab steel stall system has 60% competence overlap with Böhmer's welded steel capability. 8/10 strategic fit. EUR 150–250K investment, 12–18 months.

vnUnits.name: Bedding Management Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Manual or mechanical muck-out equipment. Wheelbarrow + fork (~60%), mechanical stall cleaner (~25%), rubber mat system (~15%).
vnUnits.description: Labor-intensive daily operation. No Böhmer overlap — no steel fabrication competence in rubber mats.

vnUnits.name: Farriery Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Hoof care tools and equipment used by farrier. Farrier stand, hoof pick, trimming tools, forge/anvil (traveling kit).
vnUnits.description: Specialist professional equipment. Farrier typically brings own tools. Facility supplies handling stock only.

vnUnits.name: Veterinary Treatment Stocks / Restraint
vnUnits.functionalJob: Physical restraint equipment for veterinary procedures. Examination stocks, restraint halter/twitch, treatment cabinet.
vnUnits.description: Standard facility equipment. Not a competitive differentiator in most facilities.

vnUnits.name: Manure Collection Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Daily dung removal from stalls and paddocks. Wheelbarrow or manure cart, loading ramp, spreader hitch.
vnUnits.description: LOW-EFFORT ADJACENCY: manure bunker/container is a welded steel box within Böhmer fabrication capability. Low unit value (EUR 200–800). 6/10 strategic fit.

vnUnits.name: Manure Storage / Mistlager
vnUnits.functionalJob: Covered storage for dung accumulation between disposal events. Manure bunker, leachate drainage, cover/ventilation.
vnUnits.description: LOW-EFFORT ADJACENCY: welded steel container with drainage fitting — direct analogy to Böhmer fabrication capability. Under Tierschutzgesetz requirements.

vnUnits.name: Lunging & Groundwork Equipment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Non-ridden exercise and training aids. Lunging cavesson, long reins, marker cones.
vnUnits.description: Low-value consumable equipment. No fabrication component.

vnUnits.name: Mechanical Walker / Hot Walker
vnUnits.functionalJob: Motorized rotating arm structure for autonomous horse exercise. Motor mount, rope attachment, timer/speed control.
vnUnits.description: HIGH adaptation effort — motor, bearing, safety-speed control integration required. Excluded from near-term Böhmer scope.

vnUnits.name: Breeding Handling Equipment (Gestüt only)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Breeding stocks, semen collection/evaluation kit, AI catheter set for assisted reproduction.
vnUnits.description: Specialist equipment for OT-B Gestüt/Breeding only. No Böhmer overlap.

vnUnits.name: Equine Passport & Feeding Log Records
vnUnits.functionalJob: Maintain individual animal records per EU Reg. 504/2008. Digital or paper feeding logs per Tierschutzgesetz. Mandatory across all core steps.
vnUnits.description: Compliance requirement; increasingly digitized (eHorse, StableMate). No steel fabrication component.

vnUnits.name: Structural Maintenance & Equipment Repair
vnUnits.functionalJob: Annual inspection of stable structures, arena fencing, paddock panels. Replace worn or damaged equipment. Tighten or re-weld structural connections.
vnUnits.description: Recurring revenue opportunity for Böhmer: spare parts supply (replacement rails, castors, hinge pins) and repair welding for existing Paddockbox/Heuraufe customers.

böhmerPosition: Böhmer products occupy two L5 PRIMARY positions with universal activation across all 5 output types: Heuraufe (L6c Feeding — multi-horse outdoor hay rack) and Paddockbox (L6d Housing — mobile modular panel system). Highest-value adjacency: prefab steel stall equipment (L5 under L6d), 8/10 strategic fit, 60% competence overlap.
